trans-unit.source: The specified channel type {0} is not supported by this channel manager.
trans-unit.target: The specified channel type {0} is not supported by this channel manager.

trans-unit.source: The MSMQ message id is not in the expected 'GUID\COUNTER' format.
trans-unit.target: The MSMQ message id is not in the expected 'GUID\COUNTER' format.

trans-unit.source: The scheme of the URI is not valid for this MSMQ address translator.
trans-unit.target: The scheme of the URI is not valid for this MSMQ address translator.

trans-unit.source: The MSMQ client package does not support building channel listeners. Use CoreWCF to host an MSMQ service.
trans-unit.target: The MSMQ client package does not support building channel listeners. Use CoreWCF to host an MSMQ service.

trans-unit.source: The native MSMQ message buffer has already been finalized and cannot accept additional properties.
trans-unit.target: The native MSMQ message buffer has already been finalized and cannot accept additional properties.

trans-unit.source: The MSMQ.Messaging assembly could not be loaded. Add a PackageReference to the MSMQ.Messaging NuGet package to your application so System.ServiceModel.Msmq can dispatch messages to the underlying queue.
trans-unit.target: The MSMQ.Messaging assembly could not be loaded. Add a PackageReference to the MSMQ.Messaging NuGet package to your application so System.ServiceModel.Msmq can dispatch messages to the underlying queue.

trans-unit.source: The value of this argument must be non-negative.
trans-unit.target: The value of this argument must be non-negative.

trans-unit.source: Failed to open the MSMQ queue at format name '{0}'. See InnerException for the native MQ_ERROR_* code.
trans-unit.target: Failed to open the MSMQ queue at format name '{0}'. See InnerException for the native MQ_ERROR_* code.

trans-unit.source: The transport channel detected an incoming message that cannot be processed and that exceeded the configured allowed retries. Refer to the inner exception for details of the failure, and the documentation for the MsmqPoisonMessageException class for further guidance.
trans-unit.target: The transport channel detected an incoming message that cannot be processed and that exceeded the configured allowed retries. Refer to the inner exception for details of the failure, and the documentation for the MsmqPoisonMessageException class for further guidance.

trans-unit.source: The MSMQ send operation failed with native error 0x{0}. See InnerException for the underlying MQ_ERROR_* code.
trans-unit.target: The MSMQ send operation failed with native error 0x{0}. See InnerException for the underlying MQ_ERROR_* code.

trans-unit.source: The MSMQ send channel is not yet implemented in this build of System.ServiceModel.Msmq.
trans-unit.target: The MSMQ send channel is not yet implemented in this build of System.ServiceModel.Msmq.

trans-unit.source: A port may not be specified on a net.msmq queue URI when using the native MSMQ transport protocol.
trans-unit.target: A port may not be specified on a net.msmq queue URI when using the native MSMQ transport protocol.

trans-unit.source: Private queues are addressed using '/private/<name>' in net.msmq URIs. The legacy '/private$' syntax is not supported.
trans-unit.target: Private queues are addressed using '/private/<name>' in net.msmq URIs. The legacy '/private$' syntax is not supported.

trans-unit.source: The MSMQ queue URI is missing the host segment.
trans-unit.target: The MSMQ queue URI is missing the host segment.

trans-unit.source: MSMQ is not supported on this platform.
trans-unit.target: MSMQ is not supported on this platform.

trans-unit.source: Timeout must be greater than or equal to TimeSpan.Zero. To disable timeout, specify TimeSpan.MaxValue.
trans-unit.target: Timeout must be greater than or equal to TimeSpan.Zero. To disable timeout, specify TimeSpan.MaxValue.

trans-unit.source: Timeouts larger than Int32.MaxValue TotalMilliseconds (approximately 24 days) cannot be honored. To disable timeout, specify TimeSpan.MaxValue.
trans-unit.target: Timeouts larger than Int32.MaxValue TotalMilliseconds (approximately 24 days) cannot be honored. To disable timeout, specify TimeSpan.MaxValue.
